description: Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets consumer electronics, software, and services. Known for the iPhone, Mac, iPad, and the App Store ecosystem.
Alphabet Inc. is the parent company of Google. It operates in search, advertising, cloud computing, AI research, and hardware products like Pixel and Nest.
Microsoft Corp. provides cloud services via Azure, productivity tools like Office 365, gaming through Xbox, and enterprise software solutions worldwide.
Amazon.com Inc. is a global leader in e-commerce, cloud computing through AWS, digital streaming via Prime Video, and artificial intelligence services.
Tesla Inc. designs and manufactures electric vehicles, energy storage systems, and solar products, with a mission to accelerate the world's transition to sustainable energy.
Meta Platforms Inc. owns and operates Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. The company is heavily investing in virtual and augmented reality through its Reality Labs division.
NVIDIA Corp. is a world leader in GPU design for gaming, professional visualization, data centers, and AI computing. Its CUDA platform powers most modern AI workloads.
Netflix Inc. is the world's leading subscription streaming entertainment service, offering a vast library of TV series, films, documentaries, and games globally.
Advanced Micro Devices designs high-performance processors and GPUs for PCs, servers, and gaming consoles, competing directly with Intel and NVIDIA.
Intel Corp. is a semiconductor giant that designs and manufactures CPUs, chipsets, and integrated circuits powering personal computers and data centers worldwide.
PayPal Holdings Inc. is a global digital payments platform enabling individuals and businesses to send and receive payments securely online and via mobile devices.
Adobe Inc. offers creative software products such as Photoshop, Illustrator, and Premiere Pro, along with cloud-based tools for digital marketing and document management.
Salesforce Inc. is the world's leading customer relationship management platform, offering cloud-based sales, service, marketing, and analytics solutions for enterprises.
Oracle Corp. provides enterprise software products and services, including database management, cloud infrastructure, and business applications used across industries.
IBM Corp. delivers hybrid cloud, AI, and consulting services. Its offerings include the Watson AI platform, IBM Cloud, and enterprise IT infrastructure solutions.
Qualcomm Inc. is a leader in wireless technology, designing Snapdragon processors used in Android smartphones, automotive systems, and IoT devices globally.
Broadcom Inc. designs and supplies a broad range of semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions for data centers, networking, storage, and wireless communications.
Micron Technology Inc. manufactures DRAM, NAND flash, and NOR flash memory chips essential for smartphones, PCs, servers, and automotive systems.
Uber Technologies Inc. operates a global platform for ride-hailing, food delivery via Uber Eats, and freight logistics, serving millions of users across 70+ countries.
Airbnb Inc. operates an online marketplace for short-term homestays and experiences, connecting hosts and travelers in over 220 countries and regions worldwide.
Coinbase Global Inc. is a leading cryptocurrency exchange platform offering trading, custody, and staking services for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and hundreds of digital assets.
Palantir Technologies builds data analytics platforms for government intelligence agencies and large enterprises, focusing on AI-driven operational decision-making.
Roblox Corp. operates an online gaming and creation platform that allows users to design, share, and play games. It hosts millions of user-generated experiences.
Robinhood Markets Inc. is a commission-free investing app offering retail investors access to stocks, ETFs, options, and cryptocurrency trading from mobile devices.
SoFi Technologies Inc. is a digital personal finance company offering student and personal loans, mortgages, investing, banking, and insurance products via its app.
